THE FUNCTION MUST HANDLE NEGATIVE NUMBERS AND RETURN THE RESULT IN ASCENDING ORDER. THE ALGORITHM WILL INVOLVE THREE MAIN STEPS: ELEMENT IDENTIFICATION, DUPLICATION REMOVAL, AND SORTING WITHOUT BUILT-IN FEATURES.

TO IDENTIFY COMMON ELEMENTS, THE FUNCTION WILL TRAVERSE THROUGH EACH LIST AND COMPARE ELEMENTS. A TWO-DIMENSIONAL ARRAY WILL BE USED TO STORE ELEMENTS FROM THE FIRST LIST, AND THEN WE WILL ITERATE THROUGH THE SECOND LIST TO CHECK FOR MATCHES. THIS APPROACH ENSURES THAT WE DO NOT USE ANY BUILT-IN LIST FEATURES FOR ELEMENT IDENTIFICATION.

*HIGHLIGHTED SECTION 3: REMOVAL OF DUPLICATES*

TO AVOID REPEATED ELEMENTS, THE FUNCTION WILL USE A SECONDARY ARRAY TO STORE UNIQUE ELEMENTS. BEFORE ADDING AN ELEMENT TO THIS ARRAY, IT WILL CHECK WHETHER THE ELEMENT ALREADY EXISTS. THIS CHECK IS DONE USING A SIMPLE LINEAR SEARCH THROUGH THE SECONDARY ARRAY, ENSURING THAT NO DUPLICATES ARE ADDED.
